Sponsors and income • Parents as main sponsors with combined income ≥ INR 12 lakhs (≥ INR 8 lakhs from one business/profession); limited support (max 25%) from grandparents, uncle/aunt, or real siblings. 2. Proof of income and funds • Strong documentation for business, salaried, and agricultural income (ITRs, Form 16, registrations, land and J forms), funds 6+ months old, large deposits and FDs supported by clear source and income capacity, and use only RBI listed banks (excluding Gramin and Finacle statements). • Agriculture income is accepted only if properly documented (ITRs last 3 years, J forms, land and ownership proofs) and must be at least INR 12 lakhs per year from a single sponsor 3. Proof of income and funds • Strong documentation for business, salaried, and agricultural income (ITRs, Form 16, registrations, land and J forms), funds 6+ months old, large deposits and FDs supported by clear source and income capacity, and use only RBI listed banks (excluding Gramin and Finacle statements). • Agriculture income is accepted only if properly documented (ITRs last 3 years, J forms, land and ownership proofs) and must be at least INR 12 lakhs per year from a single sponsor 3. Genuineness checks • Mandatory GS declaration form, Zoom interviews for high risk zones, and savings required for non collateral loan cases unless full loan disbursement is done. Please visit the university website for more information.

Sponsors and income • Parents as main sponsors with combined income ≥ INR 12 lakhs (≥ INR 8 lakhs from one business/profession); limited support (max 25%) from grandparents, uncle/aunt, or real siblings. 2. Proof of income and funds • Strong documentation for business, salaried, and agricultural income (ITRs, Form 16, registrations, land and J forms), funds 6+ months old, large deposits and FDs supported by clear source and income capacity, and use only RBI listed banks (excluding Gramin and Finacle statements). • Agriculture income is accepted only if properly documented (ITRs last 3 years, J forms, land and ownership proofs) and must be at least INR 12 lakhs per year from a single sponsor 3. Genuineness checks • Mandatory GS declaration form, Zoom interviews for high risk zones, and savings required for non collateral loan cases unless full loan disbursement is done. Please visit the university website for more information.

Successful applicants must undergo a CASA Class 1 Medical Examination by a medical practitioner known as a Designated Aviation Medical Examiner (DAME). Students will be required to hold a valid aviation medical certificate to progress through the various licencing level tests throughout the Bachelor of Aviation (Pilot Training). It is recommended that students apply for a CASA Class 1 Medical Examination prior to commencing the degree to ensure there is no existing ailment which might prevent them from completing the Commercial Pilot Licence (CPL) training or continuing their flying career to a higher level. Students are expected to source and arrange their own Aviation Medical through a Designated Aviation Medical Examiner (DAME). RMIT University does not accept responsibility for students who enrol but cannot complete the program due to an inability to receive a CASA Class 1 medical certificate. Visit CASA for more information and the location of your nearest DAME.

Sponsors and income – • Parents as primary sponsors with minimum combined income ≥ INR 12 Lakhs; maximum 3 sponsors allowed; applications from Punjab, Haryana, and Gujarat not accepted. • 2-3 years ITRs including latest year with computation sheets mandatory; ITR or Form 16 should not be recently filed; if income ≥ INR 20 Lakhs, ITRs plus 1-year bank statement along with Financial Matrix sufficient.
Proof of income and funds – • Funds assessed at GS stage and submitted via Study Link in prescribed Financial Matrix format; KC treated as Low Risk with Financial Matrix, ITRs with computation sheets, and 1-year statement. • Strong documentation required for salaried sponsors (Form 16 and salary proofs), business sponsors (GST, business proof, current account statement; MSME or UDYAM not accepted), and agriculturist sponsors (agricultural income certificate, land ownership proof, crop sale documents; entire agricultural or dairy income not fully accepted). • Minimum 6 months old savings mandatory; accepted funds include savings account, FDR aligned with income profile, education loan with collateral and valuation, PPF, EPF, GPF; unsecured loans and secured Credila loans accepted with 100 percent disbursement into nationalised bank. • In loan cases, INR 10 to 12 Lakhs savings required; minimum INR 12 Lakhs savings mandatory if education loan availed; stamped financial documents required; scholarship amount deductible; GS TAT 7 to 10 days. 
Genuineness checks - • Video interview conducted by university on case-to-case basis. • SOP and GS Questionnaire through Flinders GS form; financial documents verified via email; no separate questionnaire link sent to student.

Sponsors and income – • Parents as primary sponsors with minimum combined income ≥ INR 12 Lakhs; maximum 3 sponsors allowed; applications from Punjab, Haryana, and Gujarat not accepted. • 2-3 years ITRs including latest year with computation sheets mandatory; ITR or Form 16 should not be recently filed; if income ≥ INR 20 Lakhs, ITRs plus 1-year bank statement along with Financial Matrix sufficient.
Proof of income and funds – • Funds assessed at GS stage and submitted via Study Link in prescribed Financial Matrix format; KC treated as Low Risk with Financial Matrix, ITRs with computation sheets, and 1-year statement. • Strong documentation required for salaried sponsors (Form 16 and salary proofs), business sponsors (GST, business proof, current account statement; MSME or UDYAM not accepted), and agriculturist sponsors (agricultural income certificate, land ownership proof, crop sale documents; entire agricultural or dairy income not fully accepted). • Minimum 6 months old savings mandatory; accepted funds include savings account, FDR aligned with income profile, education loan with collateral and valuation, PPF, EPF, GPF; unsecured loans and secured Credila loans accepted with 100 percent disbursement into nationalised bank. • In loan cases, INR 10 to 12 Lakhs savings required; minimum INR 12 Lakhs savings mandatory if education loan availed; stamped financial documents required; scholarship amount deductible; GS TAT 7 to 10 days. 
Genuineness checks - • Video interview conducted by university on case-to-case basis. • SOP and GS Questionnaire through Flinders GS form; financial documents verified via email; no separate questionnaire link sent to student.
